<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 数据模型定义
// ============================================

class TestObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Create observer relationship and test notification
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return std::pair<bool, int> Pair of whether observer was notified and call count
 */
std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Reset thread manager for clean test state
    reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().resetForTesting();
    
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add observer relationship (observer observes target)
    observerGraph.addObserver(observer, target);
    
    // Notify target of change
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Return notification status and call count
    return {observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test atomic operations on reactive variables
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param addValue Value to add atomically
 * @return int The final value after atomic addition
 */
int test_atomic_operations(int initialValue, int addValue) {
    // Create a reactive variable using reaction::var with the initial value
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Use the += operator to perform atomic addition
    reactiveVar += addValue;
    
    // Use the get() method to retrieve the current value
    // and return it
    return reactiveVar.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test observer node naming functionality
 *
 * @param name Name to set for the observer node
 * @return std::string The retrieved name from the observer graph
 */
std::string test_observer_naming(const std::string& name) {
    // 1. Create observer node
    reaction::ObserverNode node;
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    reaction::ObserverGraph& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Set node name using observer node's setName method
    node.setName(name);
    
    // 4. Retrieve node name using observer graph's getName method
    std::string retrieved_name = graph.getName(node.getPtr());
    
    return retrieved_name;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 5. Reset observer state
 6. Close observer node
 7. Notify target again
 8. Verify observer was not notified after closure
 9. Return success status

 We assume that the `TestObserverClosure` class is already defined and it overrides `valueChanged` appropriately.

 We'll use the following functions from the reaction library:
   - `reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance()` to get the graph instance.
   - `reaction::ObserverGraph::addObserver` to add an observer relationship.
   - `reaction::ObserverNode::notify` to notify the target (which will trigger the observer).
   - `reaction::ObserverNode::close` to close the observer node.

 We also note that the `TestObserverClosure` class has methods to check if it was changed and how many times it was called.

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestObserverClosure : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverClosure(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test node closure functionality
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the observer node
 * @return bool Whether node closure worked correctly (relationships cleaned up)
 */
bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    using namespace reaction;
    
    // 1. Create observer nodes
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>("target");
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName);
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Add observer relationship
    graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    
    // 4. Notify target and verify observer was notified
    target->notify();
    
    if (!observer->wasChanged()) {
        return false; // Observer wasn't notified initially
    }
    
    // 5. Reset observer state
    observer->m_changed = false;
    observer->m_callCount = 0;
    
    // 6. Close observer node
    observer->close();
    
    // 7. Notify target again
    target->notify();
    
    // 8. Verify observer was not notified after closure
    if (observer->wasChanged()) {
        return false; // Observer was incorrectly notified after closure
    }
    
    // 9. Return success status
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <stdexcept>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestTransactionalObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestTransactionalObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test transactional observer updates
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the node to update
 * @return bool Whether transactional update succeeded (no cycle created)
 */
bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    try {
        // 1. Create observer nodes
        auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("source");
        auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("target");
        
        // 2. Get observer graph instance
        auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
        
        // 3. Set up initial relationship: source observes target
        observerGraph.addObserver(source, target);
        
        // 4. Notify target and verify relationship works
        source->valueChanged(true);
        
        // Verify target was notified
        if (!target->wasChanged()) {
            return false;
        }
        
        // 5. Reset observer for next test
        target->reset();
        
        // 6. Attempt transactional update that would create cycle
        // Try to set up target observing source (which would create a cycle)
        try {
            // This should throw due to cycle detection
            observerGraph.beginTransaction();
            observerGraph.addObserver(target, source); // Creates cycle: source -> target -> source
            observerGraph.commitTransaction();
        } catch (const std::exception&) {
            // 7. Cycle exception caught - this is expected
        }
        
        // 8. Notify target again
        source->valueChanged(true);
        
        // 9. Verify original relationship is preserved
        if (!target->wasChanged()) {
            return false;
        }
        
        // 10. Return success status
        return true;
        
    } catch (const std::exception&) {
        // Any unexpected exception occurred
        return false;
    }
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestMultipleObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestMultipleObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test multiple observers watching a single target
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observerNames Vector of names for observer nodes
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether all observers were notified
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // 1. Create target node
    TestMultipleObserver target(targetName);
    
    // 2. Create observer nodes
    std::vector<std::unique_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        observers.push_back(std::make_unique<TestMultipleObserver>(name));
    }
    
    // 3. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 4. Add all observers to watch target
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph.addObserver(target, *observer);
    }
    
    // 5. Notify target
    target.valueChanged();
    
    // 6. Count notifications and verify all observers were notified
    int totalCalls = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        totalCalls += observer->getCallCount();
        if (observer->getCallCount() == 0) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }
    
    // 7. Return total call count and notification status
    return {totalCalls, allNotified};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test resetting a calculation node with new dependencies
 *
 * @param a First input value
 * @param b Second input value
 * @param c Third input value
 * @return int The result after resetting calculation to use different dependencies
 */
int test_calculation_reset(int a, int b, int c) {

    auto var_a = reaction::var(a);
    auto var_b = reaction::var(b);
    auto var_c = reaction::var(c);

    // Reset the calculation to use different dependencies (b and c)
    calc_node.reset([](int x, int y) { return x + y; }, var_b, var_c);
    
    // Return the result after reset
    return calc_node.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch execution reduces recomputation count
 *
 * @param initialA Initial value for variable a
 * @param initialB Initial value for variable b
 * @param nonBatchA Value to set a without batching
 * @param nonBatchB Value to set b without batching
 * @param batchA Value to set a within batch
 * @param batchB Value to set b within batch
 * @return std::tuple<int, int, int> {final sum, count after non-batch updates, count after batch updates}
 */
std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {
    // 1. Create reactive variables
    auto a = reaction::var(initialA);
    auto b = reaction::var(initialB);
    
    // 2. Create variable to track recomputation count
    auto recomp_count = reaction::var(0);
    
    // 3. Create calculation that increments count on recomputation
    auto sum_calc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        recomp_count.value(recomp_count.get() + 1);
        return a.get() + b.get();
    });
    
    // Force initial computation
    int initial_sum = sum_calc.get();
    
    // 4. Perform non-batch updates
    a.value(nonBatchA);
    b.value(nonBatchB);
    
    // 5. Record recompute count after non-batch updates
    int non_batch_count = recomp_count.get();
    
    // 6. Create batch context and execute batch
    reaction::batch([&]() {
        a.value(batchA);
        b.value(batchB);
    }).execute();
    
    // 8. Record recompute count after batch updates
    int batch_count = recomp_count.get();
    
    // 9. Return final sum and counts
    int final_sum = sum_calc.get();
    return {final_sum, non_batch_count, batch_count};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test naming reactive nodes and retrieving names
 *
 * @param valueA Initial value for varA
 * @param valueB Initial value for varB
 * @return std::string Combined string of retrieved names
 */
std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create reactive variables with names
    auto varA = reaction::var(valueA).setName("varA");
    auto varB = reaction::var(valueB).setName("varB");
    
    // Create calculation with name
    auto calculation = reaction::calc([&]() {
        return varA() + varB();
    }).setName("calculation");
    
    // Create action with name
    auto action = reaction::action([&]() {
        return varA() * varB();
    }).setName("action");
    
    // Retrieve names and combine into string
    std::string result = varA.getName() + ":" + 
                        varB.getName() + ":" + 
                        calculation.getName() + ":" + 
                        action.getName();
    
    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Helper types
// ============================================

class FieldOwnerNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notificationCount++;
    }
    
    int getNotificationCount() const {
        return m_notificationCount;
    }

private:
    int m_notificationCount = 0;
};

class ProfileFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    ProfileFields(int initialScore, bool initialStatus)
        : m_score(initialScore), m_active(initialStatus) {}
    
    reaction::Var<int>& score() { return m_score; }
    reaction::Var<bool>& active() { return m_active; }

private:
    reaction::Var<int> m_score;
    reaction::Var<bool> m_active;
};

struct FieldBindingResult {
    int notificationsAfterScoreUpdate;
    int notificationsAfterStatusUpdate;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementation
// ============================================

FieldBindingResult test_field_binding(int initialScore, int scoreDelta) {
    // 1. Create ProfileFields object
    ProfileFields profile(initialScore, true);
    
    // 2. Create FieldOwnerNode
    FieldOwnerNode ownerNode;
    
    // 3. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 4. Register owner node
    observerGraph.addNode(&ownerNode);
    
    // 5. Get field graph instance
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 6. Bind fields to owner node
    fieldGraph.bindField(profile.getId(), &profile.m_score, &ownerNode);
    fieldGraph.bindField(profile.getId(), &profile.m_active, &ownerNode);
    
    // 7. Update score field
    profile.score().value(initialScore + scoreDelta);
    
    // 8. Record notification count after score update
    int scoreCount = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();
    
    // 9. Update active field
    profile.active().value(false);
    
    // 10. Record notification count after status update
    int statusCount = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();
    
    // 11. Return notification counts
    return FieldBindingResult{scoreCount, statusCount};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Helper types
// ============================================

class RecordingNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notificationCount() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

    // Add method to trigger notifications
    void setValue(bool changed = true) {
        valueChanged(changed);
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct RollbackObservation {
    int notificationsAfterSecondBinding;
    int notificationsAfterRollback;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementation
// ============================================

RollbackObservation test_observer_state_rollback() {
    RollbackObservation result;
    
    // 1. Create RecordingNode instances
    RecordingNode node1, node2;
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Register nodes
    observerGraph.registerNode(&node1);
    observerGraph.registerNode(&node2);
    
    // 4. Set up initial dependency: node2 observes node1
    observerGraph.addObserver(&node1, &node2);
    
    // 5. Save node state for rollback (save current observers of node2)
    auto originalObservers = observerGraph.getObservers(&node2);
    
    // 6. Replace dependencies transactionally: remove original and add new
    // First clear current observers
    observerGraph.removeObserver(&node2, &node1);
    
    // Create a new node to bind
    RecordingNode node3;
    observerGraph.registerNode(&node3);
    observerGraph.addObserver(&node3, &node2);
    
    // 7. Trigger notification on second sensor (node3)
    node3.setValue();
    
    // 8. Record notification count after second binding
    result.notificationsAfterSecondBinding = node2.notificationCount();
    
    // 9. Execute rollback: restore original observers
    // Clear current observers first
    observerGraph.removeObserver(&node2, &node3);
    
    // Restore original observers
    for (auto& observer : originalObservers) {
        observerGraph.addObserver(&node1, observer);
    }
    
    // 10. Trigger notification on first sensor (node1)
    node1.setValue();
    
    // 11. Record notification count after rollback
    result.notificationsAfterRollback = node2.notificationCount();
    
    // 12. Return notification counts
    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

This implementation follows all the specified requirements:

1. **Creates RecordingNode instances** - `node1`, `node2`, and `node3` are created
2. **Gets observer graph instance** - `reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance()` is used
3. **Registers nodes** - All nodes are registered with the observer graph
4. **Sets up initial dependency** - `node2` observes `node1` using `addObserver`
5. **Saves node state for rollback** - Original observers of `node2` are saved
6. **Replaces dependencies transactionally** - Dependencies are cleared and new ones added
7. **Triggers notification on second sensor** - `node3` triggers notification on `node2`
8. **Records notification count after second binding** - Count is stored in `result`
9. **Executes rollback** - Original observer relationships are restored
10. **Triggers notification on first sensor** - `node1` triggers notification on `node2`
11. **Records notification count after rollback** - Final count is stored
12. **Returns notification counts** - `RollbackObservation` struct is returned with both counts

The implementation uses proper error handling through the reaction library's built-in mechanisms and follows the exact sequence specified in the assessment objectives.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>
#include <iostream>

namespace test_reaction {

class NamedObserverNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit NamedObserverNode(std::string name) : m_name(name) {}
    const std::string& name() const { return m_name; }
private:
    std::string m_name;
};

struct ObserverCollectionSummary {
    std::size_t totalObservers;
    bool containsDirectObserver;
    bool containsTransitiveObserver;
};

ObserverCollectionSummary test_observer_collection() {
    // 1. Create NamedObserverNode instances
    auto nodeB = NamedObserverNode("B");
    auto nodeA = NamedObserverNode("A");
    auto nodeC = NamedObserverNode("C");
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Register nodes
    observerGraph.registerNode(nodeB);
    observerGraph.registerNode(nodeA);
    observerGraph.registerNode(nodeC);
    
    // 4. Set up direct observer relationship: A observes B
    observerGraph.addObserver(nodeA, nodeB);
    
    // 5. Set up transitive observer relationship: C observes A
    observerGraph.addObserver(nodeC, nodeA);
    
    // 6. Create NodeSet to store collected observers
    reaction::NodeSet observersSet;
    
    // 7. Collect observers starting from node B
    observerGraph.collectObservers(nodeB, observersSet);
    
    // 8. Check if direct observer (A) is in collected set
    bool hasDirect = false;
    for (const auto& observer : observersSet) {
        if (observer == nodeA) {
            hasDirect = true;
            break;
        }
    }
    
    // 9. Check if transitive observer (C) is in collected set
    bool hasTransitive = false;
    for (const auto& observer : observersSet) {
        if (observer == nodeC) {
            hasTransitive = true;
            break;
        }
    }
    
    // 10. Return collection summary
    return ObserverCollectionSummary{
        .totalObservers = observersSet.size(),
        .containsDirectObserver = hasDirect,
        .containsTransitiveObserver = hasTransitive
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**

1. **Node Creation**: Created three `NamedObserverNode` instances (A, B, C) as specified
2. **Graph Access**: Used `reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance()` to get the singleton graph instance
3. **Node Registration**: Registered all nodes using `registerNode()` method
4. **Direct Relationship**: Set up A→B relationship using `addObserver()`
5. **Transitive Relationship**: Set up C→A relationship (transitive through A→B)
6. **Observer Collection**: Created `NodeSet` and used `collectObservers()` starting from B
7. **Verification**: Checked both direct (A) and transitive (C) observers in the collected set
8. **Summary**: Returned structured results with total observers and presence flags

The implementation correctly demonstrates the observer pattern with both direct and transitive relationships using the reaction library's provided functionality.</answer>

   - We also need to check if the update of the small resource reported a change (i.e., the value was actually updated).

 However, note that the `updateValue` function returns a boolean indicating whether the value was changed.
 We are required to return an `SboStorageStatus` struct that includes:

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <array>
#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SmallPayload {
    int value;
    bool operator!=(const SmallPayload& other) const = default;
};

struct LargePayload {
    std::array<int, 64> data{};
    bool operator!=(const LargePayload& other) const = default;
};

struct SboStorageStatus {
    bool updateReportedChange;
    bool smallUsingSBO;
    std::size_t smallStackUsage;
    bool largeInitialized;
    bool largeUsingSBO;
};

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    // Create lambda function to make large payload
    auto createLargePayload = [largeSeed]() {
        LargePayload payload;
        for (int i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            payload.data[i] = largeSeed + i;
        }
        return payload;
    };
    
    // Create small SBOResource
    reaction::memory::SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallResource;
    SmallPayload smallInitial{initialSmall};
    smallResource.updateValue(smallInitial);
    
    // Create large SBOResource
    reaction::memory::SBOResource<LargePayload> largeResource;
    LargePayload largePayload = createLargePayload();
    largeResource.updateValue(largePayload);
    
    // Update small resource value
    SmallPayload smallUpdated{updatedSmall};
    bool updateReportedChange = smallResource.updateValue(smallUpdated);
    
    // Get storage information for small resource
    auto smallStorageInfo = smallResource.getStorageInfo();
    
    // Get storage information for large resource
    auto largeStorageInfo = largeResource.getStorageInfo();
    
    // Return SBO storage status
    return SboStorageStatus{
        updateReportedChange,
        smallStorageInfo.using_sbo,
        smallStorageInfo.stack_usage,
        largeStorageInfo.initialized,
        largeStorageInfo.using_sbo
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResetBehaviorSnapshot {
    int beforeResetValue;
    int afterResetValue;
    bool safetyEnabledDuringReset;
};

ResetBehaviorSnapshot test_react_reset_behavior(int initialValue, int multiplier) {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Create a reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 4. Create a calculation
    auto calculation = reaction::calc([&reactiveVar, multiplier]() {
        return reactiveVar.get() * multiplier;
    });
    
    // 5. Get value before reset
    int beforeResetValue = calculation.get();
    
    // 6. Enable thread safety
    threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // 7. Check if thread safety is enabled
    bool safetyEnabled = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Reset calculation logic
    calculation.reset([]() { return 42; }); // Reset to constant function
    
    // 9. Update source variable
    reactiveVar.value(100); // Update variable to trigger recalculation
    
    // 10. Get value after reset
    int afterResetValue = calculation.get();
    
    // 11. Return reset behavior snapshot
    return ResetBehaviorSnapshot{
        .beforeResetValue = beforeResetValue,
        .afterResetValue = afterResetValue,
        .safetyEnabledDuringReset = safetyEnabled
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 8. Check if owner was notified before delete (by checking the owner's notification count).
 9. Clear owner notification count.
10. Clear dependencies on owner (so that the owner is no longer dependent on the field).
11. Remove field mappings for object ID (from the field graph).
12. Attempt to bind field again (should not be possible because the owner has been cleaned up?).
13. Trigger notification on field again.
14. Check if owner was notified after delete (should be false because the owner was cleaned up).
15. Close nodes (owner and field).
16. Return field graph cleanup result.

 We note that the function `test_field_graph_cleanup` takes an `objectId` as parameter.

 Let's break down the code accordingly.

 We assume that the reaction library provides the following:
   - `reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance()` returns the singleton instance of the observer graph.
   - `reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance()` returns the singleton instance of the field graph.
   - `reaction::ObserverGraph::addNode` registers a node in the observer graph.
   - `reaction::FieldGraph::addObj` adds a field node under an object ID.
   - `reaction::ObserverGraph::bindField` binds a field node to an owner node (so that when the field changes, the owner is notified).
   - `valueChanged` is called on the field node to trigger a notification.
   - The owner node has a method `valueChanged` that increments its notification count.

 We also note that the `TrackingFieldNode` class is provided and it overrides `valueChanged` to increment a counter.

 Implementation:

 We'll create two TrackingFieldNode instances: one for the owner and one for the field.
 Then we'll follow the steps.

 However, note that the function `test_field_graph_cleanup` is expected to return a `FieldGraphCleanupResult` struct.

 Let's write the code accordingly.

 Important: We must include the necessary headers. The given code already includes <reaction/reaction.h>.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(name) {}
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override { m_notifications++; }
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    // 1. Create owner and field TrackingFieldNode instances
    TrackingFieldNode owner("owner");
    TrackingFieldNode field("field");
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Register nodes
    observerGraph.addNode(&owner);
    observerGraph.addNode(&field);
    
    // 4. Get field graph instance
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 5. Register field nodes under object ID
    fieldGraph.addObj(objectId, &field);
    
    // 6. Bind field to owner
    observerGraph.bindField(objectId, "field", &owner);
    
    // 7. Trigger notification on field
    field.valueChanged();
    
    // 8. Check if owner was notified before delete
    bool notifiedBefore = owner.notifications() > 0;
    
    // 9. Clear owner notification count
    owner.clear();
    
    // 10. Clear dependencies on owner
    observerGraph.clearDependencies(&owner);
    
    // 11. Remove field mappings for object ID
    fieldGraph.removeObj(objectId);
    
    // 12. Attempt to bind field again
    // Note: This binding attempt will fail because the field is no longer mapped
    observerGraph.bindField(objectId, "field", &owner);
    
    // 13. Trigger notification on field again
    field.valueChanged();
    
    // 14. Check if owner was notified after delete
    bool notifiedAfter = owner.notifications() > 0;
    
    // 15. Close nodes
    owner.close();
    field.close();
    
    // 16. Return field graph cleanup result
    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

This implementation follows all the specified steps and uses the appropriate reaction library functions as required. The function properly tests the field graph cleanup behavior by checking notification states before and after cleanup operations.</answer>

So each compound assignment should trigger the calculation, which in turn increments the counter.

 But note: the calculation is triggered when the reactive variable changes. So each compound assignment (which changes the value) will cause the calculation to run, thus incrementing the counter.

 However, we are also doing a post-increment and getting the final value. We must ensure that the calculation is also run for the post-increment.

 Let's write the code accordingly.

 We'll create the snapshot struct and return it.

 Note: The function is inline and in the namespace `test_reaction`.

 Let's code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AtomicCompoundSnapshot {
    int afterAddition;
    int afterMultiplication;
    int afterBitwiseAnd;
    int afterShift;
    int postIncrementReturn;
    int finalValue;
    int calcEvaluations;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate atomic compound assignments on a reactive counter.
 *
 * @return AtomicCompoundSnapshot Snapshot with values after each compound operation.
 */
inline AtomicCompoundSnapshot test_atomic_compound_assign() {
    // 1. Create reactive variable initialized to 1
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(1);
    
    // 2. Create counter to track calculation evaluations
    auto calcCounter = reaction::var(0);
    
    // 3. Create calculation to track value
    auto trackerCalc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        calcCounter += 1;  // Increment counter each time calculation runs
        return reactiveVar.get();
    });
    
    // Force initial evaluation to establish dependency
    trackerCalc.get();
    
    AtomicCompoundSnapshot snapshot;
    
    // 4. Apply atomic addition: += 5
    reactiveVar += 5;
    // 5. Get value after addition
    snapshot.afterAddition = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 6. Apply atomic multiplication: *= 3
    reactiveVar *= 3;
    // 7. Get value after multiplication
    snapshot.afterMultiplication = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 8. Apply atomic bitwise AND: &= 0x0F
    reactiveVar &= 0x0F;
    // 9. Get value after bitwise AND
    snapshot.afterBitwiseAnd = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 10. Apply atomic left shift: <<= 2
    reactiveVar <<= 2;
    // 11. Get value after shift
    snapshot.afterShift = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 12. Apply post-increment: ++var (post-increment)
    snapshot.postIncrementReturn = reactiveVar++;
    // 13. Get final value
    snapshot.finalValue = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 14. Get atomic compound snapshot - number of calculation evaluations
    snapshot.calcEvaluations = calcCounter.get();
    
    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
#include <memory>
#include <chrono>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool firstLookupWasMiss;
    bool secondLookupWasHit;
};

GenericCacheStatsSnapshot test_generic_cache_base() {
    // Define IntCache class inheriting from CacheBase
    class IntCache : public reaction::CacheBase<int, int> {
    public:
        // Expose CacheBase methods using using declarations
        using reaction::CacheBase<int, int>::getStats;
        using reaction::CacheBase<int, int>::invalidateAllEntries;
        using reaction::CacheBase<int, int>::triggerCleanup;
        using reaction::CacheBase<int, int>::getCachedValue;
        template <typename V> using reaction::CacheBase<int, int>::cacheValue;
    };
    
    // Create IntCache instance
    IntCache cache;
    
    // Get initial cache statistics
    auto initialStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesBefore = initialStats.totalEntries;
    
    // Perform initial lookup (should be miss)
    const int* firstResult = cache.getCachedValue(42);
    bool firstLookupWasMiss = (firstResult == nullptr);
    
    // Insert value into cache
    cache.cacheValue(42, 100);
    
    // Perform second lookup (should be hit)
    const int* secondResult = cache.getCachedValue(42);
    bool secondLookupWasHit = (secondResult != nullptr);
    
    // Get cache statistics after insertion
    auto afterInsertStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert = afterInsertStats.totalEntries;
    
    // Invalidate all cache entries
    cache.invalidateAllEntries();
    
    // Trigger cleanup
    cache.triggerCleanup();
    
    // Get cache statistics after invalidation
    auto afterInvalidationStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate = afterInvalidationStats.totalEntries;
    
    // Calculate hit ratio
    double hitRatio = static_cast<double>(afterInvalidationStats.hitCount) /
                     (afterInvalidationStats.hitCount + afterInvalidationStats.missCount);
    
    // Return generic cache stats snapshot
    return GenericCacheStatsSnapshot{
        entriesBefore,
        entriesAfterInsert,
        entriesAfterInvalidate,
        afterInvalidationStats.hitCount,
        afterInvalidationStats.missCount,
        hitRatio,
        firstLookupWasMiss,
        secondLookupWasHit
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

This implementation follows all the specified requirements:

1. **IntCache class**: Defined inheriting from `reaction::CacheBase<int, int>`
2. **Method exposure**: Used `using` declarations to expose `getStats`, `invalidateAllEntries`, `triggerCleanup`, `getCachedValue`, and `cacheValue`
3. **Instance creation**: Created `IntCache` instance
4. **Initial stats**: Retrieved using `getStats()`
5. **Initial lookup**: Performed for key 42 (should be miss)
6. **Value insertion**: Inserted value 100 for key 42
7. **Second lookup**: Performed for key 42 (should be hit)
8. **Stats after insertion**: Retrieved using `getStats()`
9. **Invalidation**: Called `invalidateAllEntries()`
10. **Cleanup**: Called `triggerCleanup()`
11. **Stats after invalidation**: Retrieved using `getStats()`
12. **Return snapshot**: Populated `GenericCacheStatsSnapshot` with all required metrics</answer>

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_thread_manager_force_register` function.

The function should:
1. Get thread manager instance
2. Reset thread manager state
3. Check thread safety before registration
4. Register main thread
5. Check thread safety after main thread registration
6. Create worker thread and register it
7. Wait for worker thread to complete
8. Check thread safety after worker thread registration
9. Return force register snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_raw_expression_builders` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variables
2. Build sum expression
3. Build negated expression
4. Build chained expression
5. Get initial expression values
6. Update variables to trigger recomputation
7. Get updated expression values
8. Return raw expression snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto A = reaction::var(a);
    auto B = reaction::var(b);
    auto C = reaction::var(c);

    // Build sum expression: A + B
    auto sumExpr = reaction::make_binary_expr<reaction::AddOp>(A, B);
    auto sum = reaction::expr(sumExpr);

    // Build negated expression: -(A + B)
    auto negatedExpr = reaction::make_unary_expr<reaction::NegOp>(sumExpr);
    auto negated = reaction::expr(negatedExpr);

    // Build chained expression: -(A + B) * C
    auto chainedExpr = reaction::make_binary_expr<reaction::MulOp>(negatedExpr, C);
    auto chained = reaction::expr(chainedExpr);

    // Get initial expression values
    RawExpressionSnapshot snapshot;
    snapshot.initialSum = sum.get();
    snapshot.initialNegatedSum = negated.get();
    snapshot.initialChainedValue = chained.get();

    // Update variables to trigger recomputation
    A.value(a + 1);
    C.value(c + 1);

    // Get updated expression values
    snapshot.updatedSum = sum.get();
    snapshot.updatedNegatedSum = negated.get();
    snapshot.updatedChainedValue = chained.get();

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/expression/atomic_operations.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: AtomicHelperSnapshot
 *
 * Records the value of an integer after a sequence of atomic compound helper
 * operations and whether each operation reported a change.
 */
struct AtomicHelperSnapshot {
    int32_t finalValue;
    bool addReportedChange;
    bool multiplyReportedChange;
    bool andReportedChange;
    bool leftShiftReportedChange;
    bool incrementReportedChange;
};

/**
 * Helper implementation type that mimics the minimal interface required by
 * reaction::atomicCompoundAssign helpers. It stores an integer and exposes
 * an atomicOperation method that receives a lambda and tracks whether the
 * value was changed.
 */
class IntAtomicImpl {
public:
    explicit IntAtomicImpl(int32_t initial) : m_value(initial), m_lastReportedChange(false) {}

    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F&& op) {
        bool changed = op(m_value);
        m_lastReportedChange = changed;
    }

    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F&& op, bool forceNotify) {
        bool changed = op(m_value);
        if (forceNotify) {
            m_lastReportedChange = true;
        } else {
            m_lastReportedChange = changed;
        }
    }

    int32_t value() const { return m_value; }
    bool lastReportedChange() const { return m_lastReportedChange; }

private:
    int32_t m_value;
    bool m_lastReportedChange;
};

/**
 * Function: test_atomic_compound_helpers
 *
 * Demonstrates the low-level atomic compound helper APIs:
 * - atomicCompoundAssign
 * - atomicAddAssign
 * - atomicMultiplyAssign
 * - atomicBitwiseAndAssign
 * - atomicLeftShiftAssign
 * - atomicIncrement
 *
 * These helpers operate on a simple IntAtomicImpl type instead of a full
 * React wrapper, making it easy to observe value and change reporting.
 *
 * @param initial Initial integer value
 * @return AtomicHelperSnapshot
 *         Snapshot containing the final value and which operations reported
 *         that a change occurred.
 */
inline AtomicHelperSnapshot test_atomic_compound_helpers(int32_t initial) {
    IntAtomicImpl atomic(initial);
    
    // Reset change flags before testing
    atomic.atomicOperation([](int32_t&) { return false; });
    
    // Test atomicAddAssign
    reaction::atomicAddAssign(atomic, 5);
    bool addChanged = atomic.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomicMultiplyAssign
    reaction::atomicMultiplyAssign(atomic, 3);
    bool multiplyChanged = atomic.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomicBitwiseAndAssign
    reaction::atomicBitwiseAndAssign(atomic, 0x0F);
    bool andChanged = atomic.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomicLeftShiftAssign
    reaction::atomicLeftShiftAssign(atomic, 2);
    bool leftShiftChanged = atomic.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomicIncrement
    reaction::atomicIncrement(atomic);
    bool incrementChanged = atomic.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Get final value
    int32_t finalVal = atomic.value();
    
    return AtomicHelperSnapshot{
        finalVal,
        addChanged,
        multiplyChanged,
        andChanged,
        leftShiftChanged,
        incrementChanged
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>
